raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "55% of likely voters in California support a $9 billion housing bond.",
    "Support for the housing bond increases to 64% when voters are reminded it could provide tens of thousands of homes to at-risk populations and would not raise taxes.",
    "California has nearly 47,000 affordable homes in the construction pipeline waiting on funding to proceed.",
    "These homes would provide housing to more than 512,000 low-income households over the next 55 years.",
    "AB 1657, in its current form, would help the state produce and preserve more than 120,000 homes.",
    "California is funding only 12% of what is needed to meet its housing goals.",
    "California needs to produce an additional 2.5 million housing units in the next decade to meet unmet housing needs.",
    "An average of 20,000 new affordable units have been built across California each year since 2020.",
    "A coalition of more than 250 community organizations has voiced support for the housing bond proposal."
  ]
}
```
